evals.name: daily-morning-digest
evals.prompt: Give me my daily digest. What do I need to know today? I have three meetings and Omar still has not confirmed the analytics brief delegation from yesterday.
evals.expected_output: A one-page daily digest with headline status (GREEN/AMBER/RED), today's critical path (3-5 items max), today's calendar with meeting context, open delegations status showing Omar's unconfirmed analytics brief, flagged items from yesterday, this week at a glance, upcoming deadlines, and weekly priorities reminder.
expectations: Digest is no longer than one page / 5 minutes to read — if it exceeds this, items are being over-included
Critical path contains a maximum of 5 items ordered by importance, not urgency
Open delegations section prominently flags Omar's unconfirmed analytics brief with a follow-up recommendation
Weekly priorities reminder section is present — daily urgency must not hijack weekly importance
Tone is warm and direct like a knowledgeable colleague, not system-style language (no 'Status: pending; Action: review')

evals.name: monday-start-of-week-digest
evals.prompt: It is Monday morning. Give me my start of week briefing. What carried over from last week and what is my critical path for this week?
evals.expected_output: A Monday variant digest with all standard sections plus 'This Week's Critical Path' (3-5 items that make the week a success) and 'Open from Last Week' (items that carried over with context).
expectations: Monday digest variant includes the additional 'This Week's Critical Path' section with 3-5 items that define a successful week
Monday digest variant includes 'Open from Last Week' section showing carried-over items
Digest stays within one-page length constraint despite the additional Monday sections
Weekly priorities reminder anchors the start of the week

evals.name: negative-should-use-progress-tracker
evals.prompt: Give me the executive dashboard with RAG status across all projects. I need the full portfolio view with milestones and blockers.
evals.expected_output: This query should be handled by progress-tracker, not digest. An executive dashboard with RAG status and portfolio view is a progress-tracker function.
expectations: This query is about an executive dashboard with project-level RAG status and milestone tracking — it matches progress-tracker triggers (executive dashboard, RAG status, portfolio view)
Digest produces a daily briefing focused on today's priorities, not a comprehensive project portfolio view

evals.name: negative-should-use-meeting-intelligence
evals.prompt: Prep me for the 2pm board meeting. I need context on each agenda item, stakeholder positions, and what was decided last time we met.
evals.expected_output: This query should be handled by meeting-intelligence, not digest. Meeting preparation with agenda context and stakeholder notes is a meeting-intelligence function.
expectations: This query is about preparing for a specific meeting with agenda items and stakeholder analysis — it matches meeting-intelligence triggers (meeting prep, before the meeting)
Digest provides a daily overview across all activities, not deep preparation for a single meeting
